content: BSI diagnosis: 1) Blood cultures before antibiotics (2-3 sets from different sites, 20-30mL per set for adults); 2) For suspected CRBSI: paired quantitative cultures from catheter and peripheral vein (≥3:1 ratio catheter:peripheral suggests CRBSI) or differential time to positivity (DTP ≥2 hours earlier from catheter indicates CRBSI). Contaminants vs true pathogens: coagulase-negative staphylococci, Corynebacterium, Propionibacterium often contaminants unless multiple positive cultures or prosthetic material present. Rapid diagnostics: PCR panels (BioFire, T2), MALDI-TOF for organism ID, molecular resistance gene detection. Imaging to identify source/metastatic infection. Procalcitonin kinetics may guide duration. Remove and culture catheter tip if removed (≥15 CFU by roll plate method significant).
